steps.title: Follow the dig sequence
steps.content: The passage lays out an archaeological dig in order: permissions are granted, a surveyor creates a grid, the site is dug, and artifacts are recorded and mapped. The blank introduces the last step, when artifacts are removed, cataloged, and analyzed.

steps.title: Reject non-sequence signals
steps.content: Choice A would introduce an example, but the sentence gives the next action. Choice B would signal contradiction, though nothing is being contradicted. Choice C would move backward in time, conflicting with the step-by-step order. The answer is D.
